meta.title: TechPulse
meta.tagline: Tech & AI news, hot takes, and who's saying what — from the builders you follow
meta.note: Built from your X Following timeline, filtered to tech/AI builders by profile. Click any story to see every take and what different people are saying.

stories.title: Next.js 16.3 Ships Instant Navigations
stories.summary: Next.js dropped a major preview bringing SPA-like instant navigation to server components. The team shared how it works — prefetching reusable route shells and caching them on the client — and devs are reacting positively.

takes.name: Next.js
takes.text:
Next.js 16.3 Preview: Instant Navigations

A new suite of tools that brings the responsiveness of client-driven SPAs to Next.js, without giving up what Server Components do best. The speed of the server, with the feel of the client.

Try it and tell us what you think ↓

takes.name: Next.js
takes.text:
So, we're borrowing a trick from single-page apps. Next.js now prefetches one reusable shell per route and caches it on the client, instead of a separate request per link.

A click renders the prefetched shell instantly while the rest of the page streams in from the server.

takes.name: Guillermo Rauch
takes.text: Next.js "Ways to fix this" with "Copy prompt" buttons is a work of (agentic) art

takes.name: Guillermo Rauch
takes.text: How we imbue coding agents with our design standards

takes.name: Andrew Clark
takes.text: Took slightly longer than I expected 😆 but I’m pretty happy with what our team came up with!

takes.name: Tim
takes.text: The demo of these changes at Next.js Nights was very well received!

stories.title: What Actually Matters When AI Writes the Code
stories.summary: François Chollet (fchollet) argues that as AI lowers execution cost, taste and architectural vision become more valuable than cleverness. Lee Robinson says evals are now a critical skill for devs who want to work in AI. The field is rethinking what it means to be a great engineer.

takes.name: François Chollet
takes.text: The true measure of a software engineer isn't their ability to write clever code. It's their ability to ruthlessly protect the codebase from unnecessary cleverness.

takes.name: François Chollet
takes.text: When the cost of execution drops, the value of taste, strategy, and architectural vision skyrockets. Previously, you were spending most of your cognitive budget on the micro. Now you are free to focus on the macro.

takes.name: Lee Robinson
takes.text:
Building high-quality evals is an increasingly important skill.

Especially if you're trying to land a job or get into AI, I'd recommend trying to benchmark models on a task/domain you care about.

If done well, you'll get the attention of any company training models.

takes.name: freeCodeCamp.org
takes.text:
Modern AI apps typically involve more than one prompt sent to one model.

In this guide, @sudheeshshetty explains how LLMs connect to tools, MCP servers, and LangChain workflows.

You'll build Node.js examples for model calls, tool use, MCP integration, and orchestration to help

takes.name: Mike Futia
takes.text:
I just built a self-improving second brain in Claude Code 🤯

A brain that runs your brand: every tool reads from it, it's wired to your live data, and it gets smarter every week.

All running on the Claude Agent SDK.

Perfect for DTC brands and agencies whose AI output sounds

stories.title: T3 Code + Grok CLI: Theo & xAI Join Forces
stories.summary: Theo (@theo) revealed that the xAI team reached out to collaborate on Grok CLI integration with T3 Code, and he's increasingly bullish on xAI. The T3 Code builder (@jullerino) shipped the integration — download T3 Code and Grok CLI to start building with your X sub.

takes.name: Theo - t3.gg
takes.text:
The xAI team working on Grok CLI has been absolutely awesome to work with. They were the ones who initiated this collab and they’ve driven it every step of the way.

Increasingly bullish on xAI and the future of Grok

takes.name: Julius
takes.text: Thank you xAI team for championing this! Download the latest version of T3Code and Grok CLI and start building with your X sub!

takes.name: Julius
takes.text: Get T3 Code today at  and Grok CLI from

takes.name: Layton Gott
takes.text: @theo I like Karpathy and I think it was deserved I mean that's seriously what Hermes and OpenClaw already is...

stories.title: GPT-5.6 Is Coming — But Access Will Be Restricted First
stories.summary: Theo (@thdxr) notes GPT-5.6 news is being framed misleadingly — it'll go into a review period with restricted access before wide release. Meanwhile devs are debating whether open source model progress can even be stopped by regulation.

takes.name: dax
takes.text:
the news about 5.6 is being phrased in a certain way

but i think it's going to be a thing where it's in a review period for a bit during which access is restricted (because it's unreviewed)

and then it'll be made public like any other model

takes.name: dax
takes.text:
the reason we excluded frontier models from our data page is they are artificially low in usage and didn't want people concluding this

most people using them use them directly, they don't buy them through us

we wouldn't be surprised if they're 90%+ of total token spend

takes.name: Kent C. Dodds 🏹
takes.text: Honest question... when open source models advance to Fable/GPT5.6 level, can the US government stop that too? Like, how long can they hold back this avalanche?

takes.name: Layton Gott
takes.text:
@OpenAI It's Codex Thursday yet there's no GPT 5.6?!

At the very least reset all our limits!

stories.title: Notion Mail & Calendar Are on the Chopping Block
stories.summary: Devs are upset that Notion might kill its mail and calendar products. Multiple people reacted to the news — some frustrated with yet another email app shutting down, others worried about Notion Calendar specifically.

takes.name: James Perkins
takes.text:
notion mail could have been great.

it was mid at best.

Notion calendar better not get chopped

takes.name: joel ⛈️
takes.text:
😭

stfg if they kill calendar... 🔪

takes.name: Ricky
takes.text: Another mail app shut down because a VC funded company can’t monetize enterprise customers enough with email

stories.title: Grok Video Dominates Vercel AI Gateway at ~50% of Traffic
stories.summary: Vercel's Guillermo Rauch reports that Grok Imagine Video has become the clear leader in Vercel AI Gateway, now generating ~50% of all developer-generated videos. Vercel also shared how they're using coding agents with design standards and evals.

takes.name: Guillermo Rauch
takes.text: Grok Imagine Video has become the clear leader in @vercel AI Gateway, now amounting to ~50% of videos generated by developers 🤯

takes.name: Guillermo Rauch
takes.text: How we imbue coding agents with our design standards

takes.name: Vercel Developers
takes.text:
To ensure coding agents live up to our design standards, we built a system with a skill, linters, evals, and an updating loop.

Learn how we did it and how to build your own ↓

stories.title: Karpathy Called Anthropic's Slack Bot a "New Paradigm" — Was He Right?
stories.summary: Andrej Karpathy praised Anthropic's Slack bot as a 'new paradigm' and got roasted for it. Theo made a reaction video sharing his thoughts, while others weigh in on whether the take was deserved.

takes.name: Theo - t3.gg
takes.text: Karpathy called Anthropic's Slack bot a "new paradigm". He got roasted for it. Was that deserved? I made a video sharing my thoughts.

takes.name: Layton Gott
takes.text: @theo I like Karpathy and I think it was deserved I mean that's seriously what Hermes and OpenClaw already is...

stories.title: Deno 2.9 Drops: 2x Faster, Desktop App, CSS Imports
stories.summary: Deno 2.9 shipped with 2x faster startup, 2x less memory usage, a Deno desktop app, CSS imports, and support for npm/pnpm/yarn/bun lockfiles. The runtime ecosystem keeps pushing forward.

takes.name: Deno
takes.text:
Deno 2.9 is released!

› deno desktop
› 2x faster startup, 2x less memory
› min-release-age by default
› css imports
› import npm/pnpm/yarn/bun lockfiles
› deno link/unlink
› deno bundle emits rolled-up d.ts

🦕 full release notes:

takes.name: Jarred Sumner
takes.text: You can also use Claude Tag to make charts.

stories.title: Tech Community Mourns Om Malik
stories.summary: Om Malik, one of Silicon Valley's most beloved writers, passed away. Theo and Guillermo Rauch paid tribute — Theo calling his pieces on the chaos and self-inflicted pains of Silicon Valley 'truly novel.'

takes.name: Theo - t3.gg
takes.text:
Om Malik passed away today. He was one of my favorite writers. His pieces on the chaos and self-inflicted pains of Silicon Valley were truly novel. I will miss hearing his thoughts.

If you haven't read this New Yorker piece, I highly recommend it. Lots we can still learn from.

takes.name: Guillermo Rauch
takes.text: @theo A really special guy. RIP

stories.title: GitHub Copilot CLI Gets Real Code Intelligence via LSP
stories.summary: GitHub shipped an LSP Setup skill for Copilot CLI that adds semantic context — definitions, references, and types — across 14 languages. Real code intelligence, not just text completion, directly in your terminal.

takes.name: GitHub
takes.text:
Bring real code intelligence to your terminal.

The new LSP Setup skill for GitHub Copilot CLI adds semantic context (like definitions, references, and types) across 14 languages.

Here’s how to get started 👇

takes.name: GitHub
takes.text:
To improve accessibility in open source, we've worked through programs, partnerships, and open tooling built with real projects and communities.

Learn more about what we've done, and what you can do to help make open source more inclusive.

stories.title: SoFi Cut Their Codebase by 60% With Flutter
stories.summary: SoFi unified their iOS and Android apps under Flutter, deleting 1 million lines of code. The team is now shipping faster with a single codebase — a compelling case study for cross-platform Flutter adoption.

takes.name: Flutter
takes.text:
Learn how SoFi reduced their codebase 60% using Flutter ✂

A unified codebase let the team delete 1M lines of code, slashing maintenance while shipping faster to iOS and Android.

Chapters: 
1:01 - The problem
1:23 - Why SoFi chose Flutter
2:17 - Results of migration
